meta.name: AIPurview AI App Trust & Transparency Index
meta.scopeStatement: Each grade reflects our analysis of what an app states in its public privacy policy and terms as of the assessment date. It measures the transparency of those documents, not the company's actual data practices, security, or compliance. Grades are our opinion, offered for general information.
meta.disclaimer: The AIPurview AI Trust & Transparency Index is provided for general informational purposes only and reflects AIPurview's good-faith analysis of publicly available privacy policies and terms of service as of the stated assessment date. Grades are opinions based on a published methodology, not statements of fact, legal advice, or an endorsement or warning about any company or product. We do not audit, test, or verify any company's actual data handling, security, or legal compliance. These documents change over time, and an assessment may contain errors or omissions. AIPurview makes no warranty as to accuracy or completeness and accepts no liability for decisions made in reliance on the index. Company and product names are trademarks of their respective owners; their inclusion does not imply any affiliation or endorsement. To request a correction, contact privacy@verifywise.ai.
meta.rankingSource: a16z Top 100 Gen AI Consumer Apps, 6th Edition (Mar 2026)
meta.enterpriseRankingSource: Menlo Ventures: 2025 State of Generative AI in the Enterprise (Dec 2025)
meta.methodologyNote: Apps are scored across 7 data-governance domains (training-data use, data-subject rights, retention, third-party sharing, transparency, sensitive data & children, security & accountability) on a 10-point additive scale, scaled to 100. Every score is anchored to a quoted clause or a recorded silence. An app with an active dealbreaker clause (sells data with no opt-out, claims a perpetual content licence, or refuses deletion) is capped at a displayed B.

apps.name: Claude
apps.summary: Claude discloses training with opt-out, user data ownership, core GDPR-style rights, multi-mechanism data sharing, and named international safeguards, but lacks concrete security controls, breach notification timelines, and AI-use transparency.

highlights.label: Training opt-out with exceptions
highlights.text: Users can opt out of training, but flagged content and explicit feedback remain used regardless

highlights.label: Data ownership and deletion
highlights.text: User owns inputs and outputs; conversations deleted from backend within 30 days of user deletion

highlights.label: International safeguards named
highlights.text: Relies on EU adequacy decisions and standard contractual clauses for transfers

highlights.label: Vague retention and security
highlights.text: Retention is 'as necessary'; security is 'appropriate technical measures' with no named controls

lastChange.changes: Re-graded under the additive 29-indicator engine (score computed in code) reading privacy policy + terms of service.

highlights.label: D2.1–5 User rights

apps.summary: Browserbase discloses no-train without opt-in for AI models, opt-out for training via account settings, and limited recipient categories, but lacks explicit access, deletion, and data-transfer safeguard mechanisms.

highlights.label: D1.2 Downgraded (zero)

highlights.label: D2.1 Downgraded (zero)

highlights.label: Core Issue
highlights.text: Browserbase policy is thin on user rights disclosure. Most indicators in D2 (access, deletion, portability, correction) are silent. D1.2 and D2.1 conflate contact email with rights mechanisms, not equivalent.

apps.name: Truveta

highlights.label: D5.1 downgrade from full to zero

highlights.label: D6.1 downgrade from full to zero

highlights.label: D1.4 now adverse

Downgraded from "half" with adverse:false to "half" with adverse:true.

highlights.label: All other awards confirmed
highlights.text: D1.1 (half): Training opt-out for generalized LLMs is conditional/named, not absolute. D2.1-D2.5, D4.1-D4.5: All cite concrete rights and named timelines or mechanisms. D3.2, D5.1-D5.5, D7.2-D7.3: Named numbers (90 days, 72 hours, SOC2/SOC3) or explicit disclosures. D3.1 (half): 'as long as necessary' is vague, ladder=0.2 correct. D7.1 (half): Names controls but no named crypto standard (AES-256 absent), only 'encrypted'. D3.3, D6.1-D6.2: Silent. D5.2, D6.3: Gated as NA.

highlights.label: Rubric conflict resolved
highlights.text: The original ledger marked D1.4 as adverse:false despite citing 'sublicensable' rights. Per rubric: 'sublicensable content licence' explicitly triggers adverse=true. This is corrected.

highlights.label: Evidence count: 25/29 quoted
highlights.text: 4 silent indicators (D1.2, D1.3, D3.3, D6.1, D6.2 = 5 silent; but D5.2, D6.3 are NA). 24 cited with evidence; 1 additional correction for D1.4 adverse flag.

apps.summary: Basis discloses conditional user rights based on location, enumerated data collection categories, and a data retention principle of "as long as needed" without numeric periods, but lacks specificity on model training, data minimization, breach notification, security certifications, and cross-border transfer safeguards.

highlights.label: User Rights (conditional)
highlights.text: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, object to, or receive a copy of personal information.

highlights.label: Data Categories
highlights.text: Account and profile data (for example, name, email, organization); Usage and log data (for example, request metadata, timestamps, error logs); Integration metadata (for example, connected provider, scopes/permissions granted); Content and data you submit or authorize through the Services; Communications with us

highlights.label: No personal data sales
highlights.text: We do not sell personal information.

highlights.label: Sharing recipients
highlights.text: We may share information with: Service providers that help us run the Services (for example, hosting, logging, or infrastructure vendors); Integration providers you explicitly authorize; Professional advisers and auditors; Regulators, law enforcement, or courts where required by law; Successors in connection with a merger, acquisition, or asset transfer.

apps.name: Hightouch
apps.summary: Hightouch discloses basic data access/correction/deletion rights, EU data transfer safeguards, and recipient categories, but silent on training-data opt-out, breach timelines, and automated-decision protections.

highlights.label: Data Subject Rights (D2)
highlights.text: Comprehensive rights to access, delete, correct, and object to processing. Portability in machine-readable format promised but deletion is conditional.

highlights.label: International Transfers (D4.4)
highlights.text: Named safeguards: EU adequacy decisions, contractual protections, and EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework certification.

highlights.label: Recipient Categories (D4.1)
highlights.text: Transparent disclosure of eight recipient categories including affiliates, service providers, advertising partners, and authorities.

highlights.label: Silent on Training & AI (D1, D5.1)
highlights.text: No disclosure of whether user inputs train models, no opt-out mechanism named, no disclosure of AI interaction points beyond reference to separate AI Addendum.

apps.name: Omni
apps.summary: Omni's global privacy policy discloses access, correction, and portability rights; shares data for ads without opt-out; and applies standard contractual clauses for international transfers.

highlights.label: Access and portability
highlights.text: You can obtain a copy of your data, request correction, and receive it in a structured machine-readable format

highlights.label: Ad sharing without opt-out
highlights.text: Personal information may be shared with third-party advertising partners for personalized ads, with no opt-out described

highlights.label: Vague retention
highlights.text: Data is kept as long as you use the service or as needed to fulfill its purpose, with no concrete period

highlights.label: International transfer safeguards
highlights.text: Cross-border transfers are supported by EU Standard Contractual Clauses

apps.name: Julius AI
apps.summary: Julius AI explicitly discloses no-train for personal data, offers 7-day deletion, no sale for ads, and age-gates at 18, but retention is vague and security claims are boilerplate.

highlights.label: No training on your data
highlights.text: The policy states it does not use your personal information to train its AI models

highlights.label: Fast deletion
highlights.text: After you cancel and request deletion, your information is deleted within seven business days

highlights.label: No selling for marketing
highlights.text: Personal information is not shared or disclosed to third parties for their own direct marketing purposes

highlights.label: Boilerplate security
highlights.text: Security is described only as industry-standard technologies and procedures with no named controls

apps.name: Nametag
apps.summary: Nametag discloses strong biometric consent controls and data deletion rights, but lacks concrete training-data opt-out mechanisms and several transparency specifics like synthetic output marking and data portability.

highlights.label: Strong training-data vagueness on D1.1
highlights.text: Policy claims anonymization and non-identifiable training but uses only adjectives ('strong focus', 'ensuring') without naming the opt-out or opt-in mechanism that triggers this protection.

highlights.label: Explicit no-sell clause (D4.3)
highlights.text: 'We do not sell your information nor will we ever do so' is an unambiguous, perpetual commitment.

highlights.label: Concrete 3-year retention with number (D3.1)
highlights.text: 'within three (3) years of your last interaction with us, whichever occurs first' provides a named, specific retention period.

highlights.label: Biometric & children protections (D6.3, D6.4)
highlights.text: Explicit consent withdrawal for biometrics and 16+ age gate with no child data selling are clearly stated.

apps.name: Snyk
apps.summary: Snyk explicitly prohibits training inputs on AI models and discloses AI use, but retains broad data retention policies and offers limited transparency on international transfers beyond standard contractual clauses.

highlights.label: Training prohibition
highlights.text: Snyk will not use (or permit any of its Affiliates, sub-processors, or other third parties to use) any Inputs to train, enhance or improve any AI Models incorporated within the Services

highlights.label: Vague retention
highlights.text: for as long as reasonably necessary to fulfill the purposes for which we collected (no concrete period)

highlights.label: Sells personal data for ads
highlights.text: We may share or sell your personal information for third-party advertising purposes (with opt-out via cookie manager or privacy@snyk.io)

highlights.label: Breach notice timeframe
highlights.text: within 72 hours, notify you of the Security Incident

apps.name: Cyera
apps.summary: Cyera discloses GDPR-compliant data rights and retention policies with opt-out controls for data sharing, but lacks transparency on AI training practices and relies on vague retention timelines.

highlights.label: Rights disclosure
highlights.text: Full rights enumeration: access, deletion, correction, portability, objection to automated decision-making with named opt-out mechanism (Cookie icon)

highlights.label: Data transfer safeguards
highlights.text: Named mechanisms for international transfers: Standard Contractual Clauses, EU Adequacy Decisions, EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) certification

highlights.label: Gap: Retention vagueness
highlights.text: Retention defined as 'as long as we need' for 'legitimate business purposes' without numeric period, though internal policy promised

highlights.label: Gap: No AI training disclosure
highlights.text: No disclosure of whether user data/inputs train Cyera's AI models; silence on model training or synthetic output marking

apps.name: Intercom Fin
dealbreakerFlags: D1.4: Customer will retain all of Customer's Intellectual Property Rights in and to the Customer Data; Intercom granted a perpetual, non-exclusive, transferable, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free license to Feedback
apps.summary: Intercom Fin's privacy disclosures are heavily regionalized, with meaningful rights (access, deletion, portability) only for EEA/UK residents; global US-default users lack deletion rights, have vague retention ("as long as necessary"), and face perpetual sublicensable Feedback licensing.

highlights.label: Perpetual feedback license
highlights.text: Feedback you provide is licensed to Intercom on a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, sublicensable basis

highlights.label: Rights limited by region
highlights.text: Access, deletion, and correction rights are described only for EEA and UK residents, leaving others without them

highlights.label: Vague retention
highlights.text: Personal data is kept only as long as necessary to fulfill stated purposes, with no fixed period

highlights.label: Boilerplate security
highlights.text: Commits only to commercially reasonable technical and organizational measures with no named standard

apps.name: Cresta
apps.summary: Cresta discloses standard data rights (access conditional, deletion with exceptions, correction unspecified), enumerated data categories, and international transfer safeguards via standard-form contracts, but lacks named AI training disclosures, breach notification procedures, and specific security controls beyond generic boilerplate.

highlights.label: Data categories
highlights.text: Policy exhaustively enumerates 12 data categories (A-L) with YES/NO collection indicators, supporting full award for D5.3

highlights.label: Access right qualified
highlights.text: Policy states 'you may have the right' to access data and says 'these rights are not absolute,' indicating conditional rather than concrete commitment, supporting downgrade of D2.1 from full to half

highlights.label: No AI training disclosure
highlights.text: Policy is silent on whether user inputs train AI models, no mechanism named, contradicting prior 'half' award for D5.1 which requires explicit AI disclosure

highlights.label: Safeguards for transfers
highlights.text: Policy names 'standard-form contracts approved by the relevant authorities' for international transfers, supporting full award for D4.4

apps.name: Regie.ai
apps.summary: Regie.ai explicitly prohibits training on user data obtained through Google Workspace APIs and discloses access, deletion, portability, and correction rights; retains data indefinitely for fraud prevention and legal compliance; shares data with named vendor categories and offers opt-out from targeted advertising; lists security controls including encryption and MFA with breach notification timeline; and has an EU GDPR representative named.

highlights.label: No-train commitment
highlights.text: Regie.ai does not use any data, including data obtained through Google Workspace APIs, to train generalized AI or machine learning models

highlights.label: Deletion rights (conditional)
highlights.text: Request deletion: Request removal of your data where applicable

highlights.label: Security controls named
highlights.text: Encryption: Your data is encrypted both in transit and at rest using industry-standard encryption protocols. Access Controls: Only authorized personnel with a legitimate business need have access to your data, protected by role-based permissions. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): Required for internal administrative accounts

highlights.label: Breach notification
highlights.text: In the event of a data breach impacting your personal information, Regie.ai will notify affected users within the legally required timeframe, following applicable data protection laws

apps.name: 11x
apps.summary: 11x restricts AI training on customer data and Google Workspace API data, secures data with database encryption and SSL, but offers limited transparency on data rights and retention periods.

highlights.label: No training on customer data
highlights.text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, 11x shall not use any Customer Data to train any artificial intelligence or machine learning models.

highlights.label: No Google Workspace data for generalized AI
highlights.text: We affirm that 11x does not use data obtained through Google Workspace APIs (including Gmail) to develop, improve, or train any generalized or non-personalized AI or ML models.

highlights.label: Customer data ownership
highlights.text: Customer exclusively owns all right, title and interest in and to the Customer Data and Performance Data. 11x shall acquire no rights in or to the Customer Data.

highlights.label: Encryption safeguards
highlights.text: All the Personal Information we have where 11x acts as a processor for a customer's data is encrypted via database encryption. All connections to our Website or Services are protected via the use of encrypted connections, such as the Secure Socket Layer (SSL) protocol.

apps.name: Common Room
apps.summary: Common Room discloses basic access, deletion, and portability rights with conditions, but lacks mechanisms for opting out of training-data use, lacks named retention periods or security certifications, and does not explicitly commit to avoiding sensitive-data collection categories.

highlights.label: Access & Rights
highlights.text: In accordance with applicable law, you may...obtain access to or a copy of your personal information...request erasure...receive an electronic copy...seek correction or amendment...object to or restrict our uses

highlights.label: No-Sell Commitment
highlights.text: For purposes of the CCPA, Common Room does not 'sell' personal information, nor do we have actual knowledge of any 'sale' of personal information of minors under 16 years of age

highlights.label: Data Categories Enumerated
highlights.text: The categories of personal information we collect include: email address, phone number, mailing address, IP address, device type, browser information, MAC address, cookie identifiers, location information, usage data, Analytics, Cookies, Pixel Tags, information from conferences, job applications, business development, professional or employment-related information

highlights.label: Children Protections
highlights.text: The Services are not directed to children under 13 (or other age as required by local law), and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we learn that we have collected a child's personal information in violation of applicable law, we will promptly take steps to delete such information

apps.name: Circleback
apps.summary: Circleback discloses basic data rights and sharing practices for its meeting automation service, with limited transparency on model training, data retention granularity, and security controls.

highlights.label: User content ownership
highlights.text: You own all right, title, and interest in your submissions, with no implied licenses granted

highlights.label: Deletion on request
highlights.text: You can request deletion through your account, and user information is deleted within 30 days of termination

highlights.label: Training use is silent
highlights.text: The policy says nothing about whether your inputs or outputs are used to train models

highlights.label: No named security controls
highlights.text: Security is described only as appropriate measures with no named mechanisms such as encryption standards

apps.name: Dust
apps.summary: Dust discloses data access, deletion, portability, and correction rights as bare commitments without concrete mechanisms, names sharing recipients and DPA contact, restricts model training via third-party providers and prohibits internal ads/marketing use, but lacks dated versioning, breach notice timeframes, and AI-interaction transparency.

highlights.label: No ads or marketing reuse
highlights.text: The policy states your data will not be used for any other purpose like advertising or marketing

highlights.label: Named retention period
highlights.text: Data is kept for the agreement duration and then archived for five years

highlights.label: Concrete security controls
highlights.text: Data is stored in an encrypted database on Google Cloud with user-specific logins and access rights

highlights.label: No effective date
highlights.text: The policy points to an effective date at the top of the page but shows no actual date or change history

apps.name: Mem
apps.summary: Mem discloses strong no-train pledges and basic access/deletion rights, but lacks versioned change notices and fully explicit output ownership terms.

highlights.label: No generalized AI training
highlights.text: We do not use your personal information, notes, or the information we collect via third parties (e.g. Google Workspace APIs) to develop, improve, or train any generalized AI and/or ML models

highlights.label: Transparent sub-processor list
highlights.text: For a current list of subprocessors, including their purpose and data location, please visit our Trust Center at trust.mem.ai

highlights.label: Named security controls
highlights.text: We encrypt all user content in transit and at rest. Mem is SOC 2 Type II compliant. 128-bit TLS encryption

highlights.label: Input ownership retained
highlights.text: As between you and Anthropic, and to the extent permitted by applicable law, you retain any right, title, and interest that you have in the Inputs you submit

apps.name: Heidi Health
apps.summary: Heidi Health discloses opt-out data training only for de-identified queries, grants core user rights with 30-day response promise, but lacks named retention periods and automated decision-making governance.

highlights.label: Training-data governance
highlights.text: PHI will not be used for model training; queries may be reviewed in de-identified form only

highlights.label: User rights enforcement
highlights.text: Access, deletion, correction, data portability, and object-to-processing requests answered within 30 days

highlights.label: Health data protection
highlights.text: Unless permitted or required by law, health information not used without explicit consent; no marketing without consent

highlights.label: Security implementation
highlights.text: Robust encryption, multi-layered encryption, stringent access controls, continuous threat monitoring named

apps.name: Outreach
apps.summary: Outreach discloses limited but reasonable baseline privacy controls, with strong rights to access and portability, but insufficient transparency on AI training and retention policies, relying heavily on vague standards like "as necessary.

highlights.label: Strong data rights
highlights.text: Access, portability, correction, and marketing opt-out are all explicitly named and available

highlights.label: Sells data with opt-out
highlights.text: It discloses CCPA sales of identifiers, network activity, and inferences, but you can opt out via a form

highlights.label: Named security controls
highlights.text: Commits to encryption of data in transit and at rest, SSO integration, and regular security reviews

highlights.label: Vague retention
highlights.text: Data is kept as long as reasonably necessary to fulfill stated purposes, with no concrete timeframe

apps.name: Abacus.AI
apps.summary: Abacus.AI explicitly prohibits training inputs on its models and maintains zero-retention agreements with third-party AI services, with clear rights to data access, portability, and correction, though post-termination retention uses vague timelines and lacks international transfer safeguards.

highlights.label: No-train commitment
highlights.text: We do not use any personal information, Google user data, or customer-provided content (including prompts, uploads, or API data) to develop, improve, or train any generalized or proprietary AI or ML models.

highlights.label: Zero third-party AI retention
highlights.text: Abacus.AI maintains a zero data retention agreement with all foundation and third-party AI models integrated into our product. Any data exchanged with such models occurs only for real-time inference and is never stored, logged, or reused by the model provider for training or product improvement.

highlights.label: Data rights
highlights.text: You have a right to request us to provide you with a copy of your personal data in electronic format... You have a right to request us to amend or update your personal data if it is inaccurate or incomplete.

highlights.label: Age gating
highlights.text: If you are under 18, you may use the Site and Services only with the supervision of your parent or guardian. The Services are not directed to anyone under the age of 16.

apps.name: Tonic.ai
apps.summary: Tonic.ai discloses moderate protections for data handling, with explicit non-selling commitments and clear age gates, but lacks named technical controls and comprehensive opt-out mechanisms for training data usage.

highlights.label: No sales commitment
highlights.text: Tonic does not sell your personal information to any third party for monetary consideration

highlights.label: Age gate
highlights.text: Tonic Services are not directed to children under 13; minimum age 18

highlights.label: Vague training data use
highlights.text: Tonic may use that data to improve its systems, products, and services (no named opt-out mechanism)

highlights.label: Generic security language
highlights.text: measures designed to protect your personal information (no named technical standards, TLS, or encryption algorithms disclosed)

apps.name: Together AI
apps.summary: Together AI discloses explicit opt-in consent for model training, user data ownership with provider reuse rights, zero-data-retention mode with instant deletion, limited recipient categories, and a named privacy contact, but is silent on data access, portability, correction, subprocessors, breach notification, and automated decisions.

highlights.label: Opt-in training only
highlights.text: Your data is not used to train models without your explicit opt-in consent, which you can revoke at any time

highlights.label: Zero-data-retention mode
highlights.text: Under ZDR, submitted content is removed from systems as soon as processing concludes

highlights.label: No sale of data
highlights.text: The policy states the company does not sell your information

highlights.label: Boilerplate security
highlights.text: Commits only to a security program in accordance with industry standards with no named controls

apps.name: Amplitude
apps.summary: Amplitude discloses limited data rights (access, deletion, portability) via email requests, vague retention ("as long as necessary"), and shares personal data with ad partners with opt-out available; it names international transfer safeguards (SCCs and three DPF frameworks) and data security controls, but lacks AI transparency, concrete breach timelines, or certification disclosures.

highlights.label: Ad sharing with opt-out
highlights.text: Personal data may be shared with third-party ad partners via cookies or email lists, with opt-out in cookie settings

highlights.label: International transfer safeguards
highlights.text: Cross-border transfers use Standard Contractual Clauses and the EU, UK, and Swiss Data Privacy Frameworks

highlights.label: Vague retention
highlights.text: Data is kept no longer than necessary for the stated purpose, with no concrete period

highlights.label: Data rights by email
highlights.text: Access, correction, deletion, and portability are available but routed through email requests rather than self-service

apps.name: Observe.AI
apps.summary: Observe.AI discloses basic data rights (access, deletion, objection contact) and limited retention guidance, but lacks concrete opt-out mechanisms, security details, and transparent data governance practices in its global privacy policy.

highlights.label: Data retention vague
highlights.text: We retain your personal information for as long as necessary to provide the services you have requested

highlights.label: Rights by contact only
highlights.text: Right to Access / Right to erase - Contact our Privacy team at privacy@observe.ai

highlights.label: Marketing data sharing undisclosed opt-out
highlights.text: Promotional and Marketing agency to send promotional emails to our customers and prospective customers

highlights.label: No security controls named
highlights.text: Observe.AI has implemented reasonable physical, technical and administrative security measures

apps.name: Fireworks AI
apps.summary: Fireworks AI explicitly commits to no-train-without-opt-in for user inputs and zero-retention for open model logs, offers full GDPR rights including deletion within 30 days and data portability, does not sell personal information, uses standard contractual clauses for international transfers, and appoints a GDPR representative; however, the terms grant Fireworks perpetual sublicensable rights over all user-generated outputs, and lacks named security controls and breach notification standards.

highlights.label: Training data protection
highlights.text: We do not use your prompts, training data, or API inputs to train or improve our AI models without your explicit opt-in

highlights.label: Output rights concern
highlights.text: you hereby expressly grant...to Fireworks a fully paid, royalty-free, transferable, perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, and worldwide license, with the right to grant and authorize sublicenses

highlights.label: Data deletion timeline
highlights.text: If you delete your account, we aim to delete your personal information from our servers within 30 days

highlights.label: Privacy representative
highlights.text: appointed GDPR Local with its local partners as our privacy representative

apps.name: Pendo
dealbreakerFlags: D1.4: grant and agree to grant to Pendo, its affiliates and their successors and assigns a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license (with right to sublicense)
apps.summary: Pendo discloses privacy rights and data practices with fair transparency but reserves perpetual sublicensable rights over user submissions, conducts training only on de-identified data with no raw-data training, limits sensitive data, and requires age 18 minimum.

highlights.label: Training data practice
highlights.text: AI/ML functionality developed only post-de-identification; no raw personal data used for model training

highlights.label: Adverse: perpetual sublicense on submissions
highlights.text: User-submitted content assigned perpetual, irrevocable, sublicensable rights to Pendo in Terms of Use Section 5

highlights.label: Strong data subject rights
highlights.text: Full access, deletion, portability, correction, and objection rights with named mechanisms

highlights.label: International transfer safeguards
highlights.text: Adequacy decisions, standard contractual clauses, and EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework compliance named
